calendar.matters:
Motion To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VALERI SHILOV'S Motion To Vacate Judgment.

Appearances:
Valeri Shilov, in person, in propria persona Defendant.
Jonathan Vaknin Esq., via Zoom videoconference, specially appearing for Christopher Stapleton, Esq., Law Office of, (310) 391-0800, for Plaintiff.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Valeri Shilov's motion to vacate judgment is denied.

Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 473, subdivision (b), allows the court to relieve a party from an "order . . . taken against the party through the party's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Relief from judgment is generally liberally construed to dispute of cases on their merits. (See Reed v. Williamson (1960) 185 Cal.App.2d 244, 248-249.) The party seeking relief "bears the burden of proof in establishing a right to relief," requiring some "satisfactory excuse for his default" and "diligence in making the motion after discovery of the default." (Hopkins & Carley v. Gens (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1404, 1410 [citation simplified].) Moreover, the statute provides, "[a]pplication for this relief shall be accompanied by a copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein, otherwise the application shall not be granted . . . ."

The court's docket demonstrates that Shilov filed an answer on August 8, 2025, making a general appearance in the case. His request for fee waiver was denied by the court on August 20, 2025. The order denying the fee waiver states "[y]ou have 10 days after the clerk gives notice of this order . . . to pay your fees as ordered . . . . If you do not pay, your court papers will not be processed." The order is accompanied by proof of mail service to the same address listed on Shilov's motion papers.

On September 18, 2025, the court struck Shilov's answer because he failed to pay the applicable fee. This order is accompanied by proof of mail service, again to the 68 Getz Street address.

Shilov's declaration states that he was aware that the case had been filed but that he was never served with any request of entry of default or any related documents. (Shilov Declaration.) But he appeared in the case, resides at the address where the court notified him his answer would be stricken and was stricken, and did not act until more than six months after his answer was stricken. The court concludes that Shilov does not present proof of excusable neglect. In addition, Shilov does not provide a copy of his proposed pleadings and does not demonstrate he has a meritorious defense. (See Bethlahmy v. Customcraft Industries, Inc. (1961) 192 Cal.App.2d 308, 310 ["It is incumbent upon one seeking relief under Code of Civil Procedure, section 473, to satisfy the court that a different result would probably ensue if the matter were to be retried."].)

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which tracks verbatim the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Motion To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Continued To Jun-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VALERI SHILOV'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The court exercises its discretion to consider plaintiff's late-filed opposition brief. Defendant Valeri Shilov has leave to file a reply brief by June 9, 2026. The motion is continued for hearing to June 16, 2026. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
